one_line: 45 analysts still at rec_mean 1.31, 79.9% institutional ownership and 1.32% short interest are unchanged despite a -27.5% drawdown — the crowd hasn't sold, it's trapped, not flushed.
evidence: Positioning data shows zero capitulation: rec_mean 1.31 (near-unanimous strong buy) and 79.9% institutional ownership are peak-consensus readings that haven't budged with the price, and analysts haven't cut targets despite a record-volume weekly reversal bar — classic denial-stage behavior, not a re-rating. Short interest at 1.32% is the tell: in a genuine flush, shorts build as bears pile onto a weakening chart; here there are effectively no bears positioned against AVGO, meaning the drawdown is being absorbed entirely by long liquidation with no short-covering cushion on any bounce and no capitulation signature (options/short skew) confirming a washout. Momentum is deteriorating but not at oversold extremes — daily RSI 39 and weekly RSI 47.5 (down from 60+) are neutral-to-soft, not the sub-30/sub-25 exhaustion readings that mark a flush's end. Layer in that AVGO is a top-3 holding in SMH/SOXX and every AI-picks list, so index/ETF-linked flows amplify both the crowding and the current unwind. Macro context (global AI selloff on dismal payrolls, Burry's public chip-ETF short, banks>chips rotation) is a top-down risk-off catalyst hitting a structurally overweight, still-fully-consensus name — pressure on a crowded trade, not evidence the crowd has already left. Testing the 200d MA with consensus/positioning unchanged means the stock is being sold out from under holders who haven't yet capitulated: CROWDED, not yet NEUTRAL (no positioning reset) and not EXTREME (oscillators aren't at capitulation extremes).
